message:
chore: sync beads and include concurrent changes

- Close bd-1p9q (linalg already committed by concurrent agent)
- Include geodesic.rs, decoding.rs, harness.rs, benchmarking docs
- Beads sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -133,7 +133,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1ows","title":"HTTP/3 client: request/response over QUIC","description":"# HTTP/3 Client Implementation

## Overview
HTTP/3 client implementation over QUIC with Cx integration, supporting
request/response patterns with proper stream management.

## Design

### H3Client
```rust
pub struct H3Client {
    conn: QuicConnection,
    h3: h3::client::Connection<QuicConnection, Bytes>,
}

impl H3Client {
    /// Create HTTP/3 client from QUIC connection.
    pub async fn new(cx: &Cx, conn: QuicConnection) -> Outcome<Self, H3Error> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        let h3 = h3::client::Connection::new(conn).await?;
        Ok(Self { conn, h3 })
    }
    
    /// Send HTTP request and receive response.
    pub async fn request(
        &mut self,
        cx: &Cx,
        req: Request<()>,
    ) -> Outcome<Response<H3Body>, H3Error> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        
        // Send request headers
        let mut stream = self.h3.send_request(req).await?;
        
        // Send body if present
        stream.send_data(/* body */).await?;
        stream.finish().await?;
        
        // Receive response
        let resp = stream.recv_response().await?;
        
        // Create body reader
        let body = H3Body::new(stream);
        
        Ok(resp.map(|_| body))
    }
    
    /// Send request with body.
    pub async fn request_with_body(
        &mut self,
        cx: &Cx,
        req: Request<Bytes>,
    ) -> Outcome<Response<H3Body>, H3Error>;
}
```

### H3Body
```rust
pub struct H3Body {
    stream: h3::client::RequestStream<QuicRecvStream, Bytes>,
    done: bool,
}

impl H3Body {
    /// Read next chunk of response body.
    pub async fn chunk(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Option<Bytes>, H3Error> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        
        if self.done {
            return Ok(None);
        }
        
        match self.stream.recv_data().await? {
            Some(data) => Ok(Some(data)),
            None => {
                self.done = true;
                Ok(None)
            }
        }
    }
    
    /// Read entire body.
    pub async fn collect(mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Bytes, H3Error>;
}
```

## Cancellation Semantics
- Request cancellation resets the stream
- Partial responses are discarded
- Connection remains usable for other requests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create H3 client from QUIC connection
- [ ] Send GET requests
- [ ] Send POST requests with body
- [ ] Receive response headers
- [ ] Stream response body
- [ ] Cancellation resets stream
- [ ] Unit tests
- [ ] Integration tests

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `h3_client::tests::create_client` - Create from QUIC
- `h3_client::tests::get_request` - Simple GET
- `h3_client::tests::post_request` - POST with body
- `h3_client::tests::stream_body` - Stream response body
- `h3_client::tests::collect_body` - Collect full body

### Cancel-Correctness Tests
- `h3_client::cancel::cancel_request` - Cancel mid-request
- `h3_client::cancel::cancel_body_read` - Cancel during body
- `h3_client::cancel::connection_survives` - Other requests work

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Frame details
- DEBUG: Request/response flow
- INFO: Request lifecycle
- WARN: Stream errors
- ERROR: Connection errors","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:23:10.541874217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:20.526333339Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:20.526269841Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","http","quic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ows","depends_on_id":"bd-2tnl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ows","depends_on_id":"bd-2vik","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1p8g","title":"[AA][Sched] Hot‑path allocation audit + arena plan","description":"## Goal
Audit hot‑path allocations and design an arena/slab strategy for zero‑alloc scheduling.

## Why
Latency and throughput are dominated by allocations on the run queue and cancellation paths.

## Deliverables
- Allocation census of scheduler hot path.
- Arena/slab plan with reuse and capacity bounds.
- Benchmarks showing allocation reduction.

## Acceptance
- No per‑poll allocation on the hot path.
- Allocation counts reduced by ≥90% on scheduler benchmarks.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealCreek","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:07:32.231390831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:17:23.122352393Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p8g","depends_on_id":"bd-1d5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:11:56.778530759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1p8g","depends_on_id":"bd-1iva","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:07:32.239063376Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1p8g","depends_on_id":"bd-ekf6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:11:56.726222915Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Structured logging with request lifecycle phase markers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:13:45.998389158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:17:28.622428698Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T19:17:28.622359549Z","close_reason":"Added 31 full-stack HTTP E2E tests in tests/http_e2e.rs: basic req/res, POST+JSON, method not allowed, 404, codec roundtrip, malformed requests, headers/body size limits, keep-alive config, concurrent routing, stress (500 requests), compression negotiation, pool lifecycle, body types, full CRUD pipeline, chunked encoding, extractor edge cases, header map ops, server config.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","http","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1phl","depends_on_id":"bd-1dgr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-1phl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Every test function must: (1) call init_test_logging() at start; (2) use test_phase!("test_name") for the top-level test marker; (3) use test_section!("phase") for each distinct phase (setup, execution, verification, teardown); (4) use assert_with_log!() instead of bare assert!() for all assertions that compare values; (5) call test_complete!("test_name") on success; (6) log key intermediate state (e.g., connection count, region tree depth, bytes transferred) at INFO level with tracing::info!() so failures can be diagnosed from log output alone.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:29:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q6r","title":"[BLOCKER] Fix transport/router.rs compilation errors","description":"The main library does not compile. cargo check --lib fails with 9 errors in src/transport/router.rs.

Key issues:
1. dyn SymbolSink doesn't implement Debug (line 843)
2. Uses self.table instead of self.router.table() (lines 997-999, 1043)
3. Returns RoutingError when function expects DispatchError (lines 1000-1003, 1014, 1058)

Impact: All integration tests blocked, no agents can verify work.

Fix: Manual Debug impl, correct field access, wrap errors in DispatchError::RoutingFailed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:47.274825603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:20:51.948345676Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:20:51.948183230Z","close_reason":"Compilation errors fixed - cargo check passes with only warnings","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-32n6","title":"[ALIEN][E2E][HOMO] End-to-end: topology-guided exploration run + coverage report","description":"## Goal

End-to-end validation that persistent-homology scoring (topological novelty) improves schedule exploration *as a user experiences it*:
- explorer runs deterministically
- prioritization is explainable (evidence ledger)
- we get better bug-finding / coverage per explored node than baseline ordering

This harness complements unit tests for complexes/persistence; it validates the whole pipeline.

## Inputs

- A fixed corpus of deterministic lab scenarios (same as / compatible with bd-1ny4)
- ExplorerConfig knobs:
  - baseline: FIFO / existing ordering
  - topo: PH-guided ordering

## Required outputs (deterministic artifacts)

- A report per scenario and aggregate summary (JSON preferred) including:
  - explored nodes until first violation (if any)
  - equivalence-class count discovered
  - novelty score distribution over time
  - top-k "most novel" nodes with ledger snippets
  - runtime overhead attribution (time spent scoring vs executing)

## Logging constraints

- No stdout/stderr from core runtime code.
- Use trace events (Cx::trace) for internal scoring decisions; the harness may print a summary.

## Acceptance criteria

- Deterministic: identical outputs across repeated runs (same seed/config).
- Demonstrates a measurable improvement on at least one scenario (fewer nodes to failure OR higher coverage at same budget).
- Includes failure-mode reporting when scoring is disabled/falls back (e.g., analysis too expensive).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:03.025357317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:13.544159309Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","e2e","lab","testing","topology","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-115y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-1ny4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-3490","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-3oqu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-453l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Scenarios: HTTP + gRPC sharing runtime, WebSocket upgrade from HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2 + pool + TLS, concurrent TCP + UDP, timer-driven timeouts during I/O, graceful shutdown across all protocols. Blocked on transport unit tests (bd-1qxg) and E2E logging (bd-1mgn) because multi-protocol tests need both foundations.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:15:12Z"},{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-32ot","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Every test function must: (1) call init_test_logging() at start; (2) use test_phase!("test_name") for the top-level test marker; (3) use test_section!("phase") for each distinct phase (setup, execution, verification, teardown); (4) use assert_with_log!() instead of bare assert!() for all assertions that compare values; (5) call test_complete!("test_name") on success; (6) log key intermediate state (e.g., connection count, region tree depth, bytes transferred) at INFO level with tracing::info!() so failures can be diagnosed from log output alone.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:29:52Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3490","title":"[ALIEN][PERF] Golden traces + isomorphism proof harness","description":"## Purpose

Extreme optimization requires *behavior proofs*, not intuition.

We need stable golden artifacts that let us assert: "after optimization, behavior is isomorphic" (same observable outcomes, same determinism guarantees, same tie-breaking).

## Deliverables

- A golden-trace fixture format (minimal, stable across versions):
  - seed/config
  - trace fingerprint
  - selected canonicalized trace (or prefix) for sanity
  - invariant-oracle results
- A checksum/verification helper used by tests/benches (deterministic, no ambient state).

## Acceptance criteria

- Can add a golden fixture for a test program and verify it deterministically.
- Failing verification produces actionable diff output (which part changed: fingerprint, event count, etc.).
- No new dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:10:08.447683662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:51:58.011219567Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:51:58.011143115Z","close_reason":"Golden trace fixture format (GoldenTraceFixture) implemented in src/trace/format.rs with: seed/config snapshot, canonical fingerprint, Foata-layer prefix, oracle violation summary. Verification helper (GoldenTraceDiff) produces actionable field-level diffs. Tested in tests/golden_outputs.rs with deterministic lab workloads. No new dependencies.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","performance","testing","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3490","depends_on_id":"bd-1i4s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-34qq","title":"Package dashboard as self-contained HTML","description":"# Task

Package the dashboard as a self-contained HTML file that can be served by the
debug server or opened standalone for post-mortem analysis.

## Requirements

1. **Single file**: All JS/CSS inlined
2. **No external deps**: Works offline
3. **Small size**: Target < 100KB minified
4. **Lab export**: Can load trace JSON file
5. **Live mode**: Connects to WebSocket when available

## Structure

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <title>Asupersync Debug Dashboard</title>
  <style>
    /* Inline CSS */
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <div id="app">
    <div id="header">...</div>
    <div id="tree-view">...</div>
    <div id="timeline">...</div>
    <div id="details">...</div>
  </div>
  <script>
    // Inline JS
    // Tree view component
    // Timeline component
    // WebSocket client
    // File loader for post-mortem
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

## Build Process

1. Write components as separate files during development
2. Build step bundles into single HTML
3. Minify JS/CSS
4. Embed in Rust binary as `include_str!`

## Rust Integration

```rust
const DASHBOARD_HTML: &str = include_str!("../assets/dashboard.html");

fn serve_dashboard(req: Request) -> Response {
    Response::builder()
        .header("Content-Type", "text/html")
        .body(DASHBOARD_HTML)
}
```

## Post-Mortem Mode

When opened as a file:
1. Prompt to load trace JSON
2. Parse and display full history
3. Allow navigation through timeline
4. Export selected events

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single HTML file < 100KB
- [ ] Works in modern browsers
- [ ] Works offline
- [ ] Live mode connects to WS
- [ ] Post-mortem loads trace files
- [ ] Embedded in Rust binary
- [ ] Responsive design","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:57:43.346942915Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:55.019698201Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:55.019550246Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-34qq","depends_on_id":"bd-4unk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:52Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-34qq","depends_on_id":"bd-9tda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:52Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-34qq","depends_on_id":"bd-nu52","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:01:52Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35f9","title":"Scheduler: prevent duplicate scheduling across local/global queues","description":"# Bug: Task Can Be Scheduled in Both Local and Global Queue

## Location
`src/runtime/scheduler/priority.rs:131-135`
`src/runtime/scheduler/global_injector.rs` (inject_ready)

## Issue Description

Deduplication only works within a single `PriorityScheduler` instance:

```rust
pub fn schedule(&mut self, task: TaskId, priority: u8) {
    if self.scheduled.insert(task) {  // Only prevents duplicates locally
        insert_by_priority(&mut self.ready_lane, SchedulerEntry { task, priority });
    }
}
```

But tasks can be injected via multiple paths:
- `schedule_local()` → local PriorityScheduler
- `inject_ready()` → GlobalInjector

No cross-queue deduplication exists.

## Duplicate Scheduling Scenario

```
1. Task T1 calls spawn() on task T2
2. T2 injected to local scheduler (fast path)
3. Before T2 executes, T1's waker wakes T2 again
4. T2 injected to global queue (waker path)
5. T2 now in BOTH local and global queues
6. Worker A pops T2 from local, executes
7. Worker B steals T2 from global, executes
8. T2 executed twice!
```

## Impact

- **Severity**: HIGH (correctness violation)
- **Exploitability**: Triggered by concurrent wake patterns
- **Impact**: Double execution, potential use-after-free

## Root Cause

No centralized "is this task scheduled?" check. Each queue maintains 
its own set, but they don't communicate.

## Proposed Fix

Centralized scheduling state in the task record:

```rust
enum TaskScheduleState {
    NotScheduled,
    ScheduledLocal(WorkerId),
    ScheduledGlobal,
    Running(WorkerId),
    Completed,
}

struct TaskRecord {
    // ...
    schedule_state: AtomicU8,  // Or use enum with AtomicCell
}

fn try_schedule(&self, task_id: TaskId, target: ScheduleTarget) -> bool {
    let record = self.get_task(task_id);
    // CAS from NotScheduled to Scheduled
    record.schedule_state.compare_exchange(
        NotScheduled,
        target.to_state(),
        Ordering::AcqRel,
        Ordering::Acquire,
    ).is_ok()
}
```

---

## Comprehensive Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (tests/scheduler/dedup_tests.rs)

#### Basic Deduplication Tests
1. **test_schedule_once_local**: Task scheduled to local once, can't reschedule
2. **test_schedule_once_global**: Task scheduled to global once, can't reschedule
3. **test_schedule_local_blocks_global**: Local schedule prevents global schedule
4. **test_schedule_global_blocks_local**: Global schedule prevents local schedule
5. **test_running_blocks_schedule**: Running task cannot be rescheduled

#### State Transition Tests
6. **test_state_not_scheduled_to_local**: Valid transition
7. **test_state_not_scheduled_to_global**: Valid transition
8. **test_state_scheduled_to_running**: Valid on pop
9. **test_state_running_to_not_scheduled**: Valid on completion
10. **test_state_completed_rejects_all**: Completed tasks never rescheduled

#### Race Condition Tests
11. **test_concurrent_schedule_same_task**: Only one succeeds
12. **test_wake_during_poll**: Wake during poll deferred properly
13. **test_wake_after_complete**: Wake after complete ignored
14. **test_stolen_task_not_duplicated**: Steal doesn't duplicate

#### Execution Count Tests
15. **test_task_executes_exactly_once**: Single wake = single execution
16. **test_multiple_wakes_single_execution**: Many wakes = still one execution
17. **test_rewake_after_complete_ok**: New wake after complete creates new execution

### Stress Tests (tests/scheduler/dedup_stress.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn stress_concurrent_wakes_single_execution() {
    init_test_logging();
    
    let scheduler = Arc::new(Scheduler::new(4));
    let exec_counts = Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new()));
    
    // Create tasks that can be woken concurrently
    let tasks: Vec<TaskId> = (0..1000).map(|_| {
        scheduler.spawn(|| {
            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_micros(100));
        })
    }).collect();
    
    // Concurrently wake all tasks multiple times
    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..10).map(|_| {
        let sched = Arc::clone(&scheduler);
        let task_ids = tasks.clone();
        thread::spawn(move || {
            for &task in &task_ids {
                sched.wake(task);
            }
        })
    }).collect();
    
    for h in handles { h.join().unwrap(); }
    scheduler.wait_idle();
    
    // Verify each task executed exactly once
    let counts = exec_counts.lock().unwrap();
    for task in &tasks {
        let count = counts.get(task).copied().unwrap_or(0);
        assert_eq!(count, 1, "task {:?} executed {} times, expected 1", task, count);
    }
}

#[test]
fn stress_wake_during_poll_race() {
    // Task that wakes itself during poll
    for _ in 0..1000 {
        let scheduler = Scheduler::new(4);
        let wake_count = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
        let poll_count = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
        
        let wc = Arc::clone(&wake_count);
        let pc = Arc::clone(&poll_count);
        
        let task = scheduler.spawn(move |waker: &Waker| {
            pc.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
            
            // Wake self during poll
            if wc.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) < 10 {
                waker.wake_by_ref();
                Poll::Pending
            } else {
                Poll::Ready(())
            }
        });
        
        scheduler.wait_task(task);
        
        // Should poll exactly 11 times (10 pending + 1 ready)
        assert_eq!(poll_count.load(Ordering::SeqCst), 11);
    }
}

#[test]
fn stress_steal_race_no_duplicate() {
    let scheduler = Scheduler::new(8);
    let executed = Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new()));
    
    for i in 0..10_000 {
        let exec = Arc::clone(&executed);
        scheduler.inject_to_worker(0, move || {
            let mut set = exec.lock().unwrap();
            assert!(!set.contains(&i), "task {} executed twice", i);
            set.insert(i);
        });
    }
    
    // Force stealing by keeping worker 0 busy
    scheduler.inject_to_worker(0, || {
        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
    });
    
    scheduler.wait_idle();
    
    assert_eq!(executed.lock().unwrap().len(), 10_000);
}
```

### Loom Concurrency Tests (tests/scheduler/dedup_loom.rs)

```rust
use loom::sync::{Arc, atomic::{AtomicU8, AtomicUsize, Ordering}};
use loom::thread;

const NOT_SCHEDULED: u8 = 0;
const SCHEDULED: u8 = 1;
const RUNNING: u8 = 2;

#[test]
fn loom_schedule_race() {
    loom::model(|| {
        let state = Arc::new(AtomicU8::new(NOT_SCHEDULED));
        let scheduled_count = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
        
        // Two threads try to schedule
        let s1 = Arc::clone(&state);
        let c1 = Arc::clone(&scheduled_count);
        let t1 = thread::spawn(move || {
            if s1.compare_exchange(NOT_SCHEDULED, SCHEDULED, Ordering::AcqRel, Ordering::Acquire).is_ok() {
                c1.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
                true
            } else {
                false
            }
        });
        
        let s2 = Arc::clone(&state);
        let c2 = Arc::clone(&scheduled_count);
        let t2 = thread::spawn(move || {
            if s2.compare_exchange(NOT_SCHEDULED, SCHEDULED, Ordering::AcqRel, Ordering::Acquire).is_ok() {
                c2.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
                true
            } else {
                false
            }
        });
        
        let r1 = t1.join().unwrap();
        let r2 = t2.join().unwrap();
        
        // Exactly one should succeed
        assert_ne!(r1, r2, "both returned same value");
        assert_eq!(scheduled_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 1);
    });
}

#[test]
fn loom_wake_during_poll() {
    loom::model(|| {
        let state = Arc::new(AtomicU8::new(RUNNING));
        let wake_pending = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));
        
        // Poller thread
        let s1 = Arc::clone(&state);
        let w1 = Arc::clone(&wake_pending);
        let poller = thread::spawn(move || {
            // Finish poll
            if w1.swap(false, Ordering::AcqRel) {
                // Wake was pending, reschedule
                s1.store(SCHEDULED, Ordering::Release);
                true
            } else {
                s1.store(NOT_SCHEDULED, Ordering::Release);
                false
            }
        });
        
        // Waker thread
        let s2 = Arc::clone(&state);
        let w2 = Arc::clone(&wake_pending);
        let waker = thread::spawn(move || {
            if s2.load(Ordering::Acquire) == RUNNING {
                // Task is running, set pending flag
                w2.store(true, Ordering::Release);
                true
            } else if s2.compare_exchange(NOT_SCHEDULED, SCHEDULED, Ordering::AcqRel, Ordering::Acquire).is_ok() {
                // Was not scheduled, schedule now
                true
            } else {
                false
            }
        });
        
        let rescheduled = poller.join().unwrap();
        let woke = waker.join().unwrap();
        
        // Wake should have effect - either rescheduled or pending
        // No wake should be lost
    });
}

#[test]
fn loom_local_global_race() {
    loom::model(|| {
        let state = Arc::new(AtomicU8::new(NOT_SCHEDULED));
        let local_queue = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));
        let global_queue = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));
        
        // Local injector
        let s1 = Arc::clone(&state);
        let l1 = Arc::clone(&local_queue);
        let t1 = thread::spawn(move || {
            if s1.compare_exchange(NOT_SCHEDULED, SCHEDULED, Ordering::AcqRel, Ordering::Acquire).is_ok() {
                l1.lock().unwrap().push(42);
            }
        });
        
        // Global injector
        let s2 = Arc::clone(&state);
        let g2 = Arc::clone(&global_queue);
        let t2 = thread::spawn(move || {
            if s2.compare_exchange(NOT_SCHEDULED, SCHEDULED, Ordering::AcqRel, Ordering::Acquire).is_ok() {
                g2.lock().unwrap().push(42);
            }
        });
        
        t1.join().unwrap();
        t2.join().unwrap();
        
        // Task should be in exactly one queue
        let in_local = !local_queue.lock().unwrap().is_empty();
        let in_global = !global_queue.lock().unwrap().is_empty();
        assert!(in_local ^ in_global, "task in both or neither queue");
    });
}
```

            std::thread::spawn(move || {
                for w in &ws {
                    w.wake_by_ref();
                }
            })
        }).collect();
        
        for h in handles { h.join().unwrap(); }
        
        // All tasks complete
        for _ in &wakers {
            // Tasks should complete normally
        }
    });
}

 4. Update with recorded values

+## Opportunity Matrix (Scoring Performance Work)
+
+Before implementing a performance optimization, score it to ensure the work is worthwhile.
+Only pursue opportunities with **Score >= 2.0**.
+
+### Opportunity Scoring Formula
+
+```
+Score = (Impact × Confidence) / Effort
+```
+
+Where:
+- **Impact** (1-5): How much improvement is expected?
+  - 1 = Micro-optimization (<5% improvement)
+  - 2 = Minor improvement (5-15%)
+  - 3 = Moderate improvement (15-30%)
+  - 4 = Significant improvement (30-50%)
+  - 5 = Major improvement (>50% or unlocks new capability)
+
+- **Confidence** (0.2-1.0): How certain are we this will work?
+  - 0.2 = Speculative (no profiling data)
+  - 0.4 = Low (indirect evidence, similar patterns worked elsewhere)
+  - 0.6 = Medium (profiling shows hotspot, approach is standard)
+  - 0.8 = High (profiling + prototype confirms improvement)
+  - 1.0 = Certain (direct measurement, trivial change)
+
+- **Effort** (1-5): How much work is required?
+  - 1 = Trivial (config change, one-line fix)
+  - 2 = Small (localized change, <100 lines)
+  - 3 = Medium (multiple files, <500 lines)
+  - 4 = Large (architectural change, new module)
+  - 5 = Major (cross-cutting, affects many subsystems)
+
+### Scoring Examples
+
+| Opportunity | Impact | Confidence | Effort | Score | Decision |
+|-------------|--------|------------|--------|-------|----------|
+| Pre-size BinaryHeap lanes | 3 | 0.8 | 1 | 2.4 | Implement |
+| Arena-backed task nodes | 4 | 0.6 | 3 | 0.8 | Needs more evidence |
+| Intrusive queues | 4 | 0.6 | 4 | 0.6 | Not yet worthwhile |
+| Reuse steal_batch Vec | 2 | 1.0 | 1 | 2.0 | Implement |
+| SIMD for RaptorQ GF ops | 5 | 0.4 | 3 | 0.67 | Profile first |
+
+### Opportunity Template
+
+Use this template when proposing a performance optimization:
+
+```markdown
+## Opportunity: [Short description]
+
+**Bead ID:** bd-XXXX (if applicable)
+
+### Scoring
+
+| Factor | Value | Rationale |
+|--------|-------|-----------|
+| Impact | X | [Why this score] |
+| Confidence | X.X | [Evidence: profile data, prototype, literature] |
+| Effort | X | [Scope of change] |
+| **Score** | **X.X** | Impact × Confidence / Effort |
+
+### Baseline Metrics
+
+| Metric | Current | Target | Measurement |
+|--------|---------|--------|-------------|
+| p50 latency | X ns | Y ns | `cargo bench --bench <name>` |
+| p99 latency | X ns | Y ns | |
+| Allocations/op | X | Y | heaptrack or alloc_census |
+| Throughput | X/s | Y/s | |
+
+### Profile Evidence
+
+[Paste flamegraph highlights, massif summary, or code review notes]
+
+### Approach
+
+[1-2 paragraphs describing the change]
+
+### Risks
+
+- [ ] Determinism: [How ordering/RNG/tie-breaks are preserved]
+- [ ] Correctness: [What invariants could be affected]
+- [ ] Complexity: [Maintenance burden]
+
+### One Lever Rule
+
+This change touches exactly one lever:
+- [ ] Allocation reduction
+- [ ] Cache locality
+- [ ] Algorithm complexity
+- [ ] Parallelism
+- [ ] Lock contention
+- [ ] Other: [specify]
+```
+
+### Policy: One Lever Per Commit
+
+Each perf commit should touch **exactly one optimization lever**:
+- Allocation reduction
+- Cache locality improvement
+- Algorithm complexity reduction
+- Parallelism increase
+- Lock contention reduction
+
+This makes regressions bisectable and improvements attributable. If a change requires
+multiple levers, split into separate commits with independent measurements.
+
+### Opportunity Tracking
+
+Track scored opportunities in the beads system with labels:
+- `perf-scored`: Has been scored using this framework
+- `perf-approved`: Score >= 2.0, ready to implement
+- `perf-needs-evidence`: Score < 2.0 but promising; needs profiling
+
+Use `br list --label=perf-approved` to find ready perf work.
+
+---
+
 ## Isomorphism Proof Template (required for perf changes)
 
 Any performance-focused change must include a **proof-of-equivalence** block.
